title: Nexus Dashboard Site and Fabric Assurance Health Score
app: Cisco DC Networking Application (Splunkbase 7777), Nexus Dashboard REST export
spl:
index=cisco_dc sourcetype="cisco:nd:site_health" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(assurance_score) as score latest(risk_level) as risk latest(open_findings) as findings by site_name, fabric_name
| where score < 85 OR match(lower(risk),"(?i)high|critical")
| sort score
| table site_name, fabric_name, score, risk, findings
description: Site-level assurance scores roll up connectivity, best-practice violations, and capacity risk across all managed switches. Surfacing declining scores in Splunk gives data center operators a single trend line to prioritize remediation before Insights opens critical anomalies during business peaks.
value: Site-level assurance scores roll up connectivity, best-practice violations, and capacity risk across all managed switches. Surfacing declining scores in Splunk gives data center operators a single trend line to prioritize remediation before Insights opens critical anomalies during business peaks.
implementation: (1) Schedule API pull from Nexus Dashboard Assurance or ingest pre-aggregated JSON via HEC. (2) Map score scale to your SLA colors. (3) Correlate drops with NDI anomalies (UC-18.4.1) and compliance drift (UC-18.4.2).
visualization: Single value (worst site score), Bar chart (score by fabric), Table (sites below threshold).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Cisco DC Networking Application (Splunkbase 7777), Nexus Dashboard REST export.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_dc` `sourcetype="cisco:nd:site_health"` with fields `site_name`, `fabric_name`, `assurance_score`, `risk_level`, `open_findings`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Schedule API pull from Nexus Dashboard Assurance or ingest pre-aggregated JSON via HEC. (2) Map score scale to your SLA colors. (3) Correlate drops with NDI anomalies (UC-18.4.1) and compliance drift (UC-18.4.2).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_dc sourcetype="cisco:nd:site_health" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(assurance_score) as score latest(risk_level) as risk latest(open_findings) as findings by site_name, fabric_name
| where score < 85 OR match(lower(risk),"(?i)high|critical")
| sort score
| table site_name, fabric_name, score, risk, findings
```

Understanding this SPL

**Nexus Dashboard Site and Fabric Assurance Health Score** — Site-level assurance scores roll up connectivity, best-practice violations, and capacity risk across all managed switches. Surfacing declining scores in Splunk gives data center operators a single trend line to prioritize remediation before Insights opens critical anomalies during business peaks.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_dc` `sourcetype="cisco:nd:site_health"` with fields `site_name`, `fabric_name`, `assurance_score`, `risk_level`, `open_findings`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Cisco DC Networking Application (Splunkbase 7777), Nexus Dashboard REST export. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_dc; **sourcetype**: cisco:nd:site_health. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_dc, sourcetype="cisco:nd:site_health", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by site_name, fabric_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where score < 85 OR match(lower(risk),"(?i)high|critical")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Nexus Dashboard Site and Fabric Assurance Health Score**): table site_name, fabric_name, score, risk, findings


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm results in the index, then compare with the same scope in the relevant fabric/SDN controller or manager UI.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (worst site score), Bar chart (score by fabric), Table (sites below threshold).
grandmaExplanation: Site-level assurance scores roll up connectivity, best-practice violations, and capacity risk across all managed switches — so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
knownFalsePositives: Faults during planned maintenance, hardware refresh, or after firmware upgrades.
